title: Topics in Population Bioethics
catalogDescription: Introduces students to bioethical questions that arise in public health, population health, and global health, situating ethical questions and challenges within a broader social context and perspective. Students interact in a small-scale learning environment with classmates and faculty and have the opportunity to learn more about the Bioethics Minor. Credit/no-credit only. Offered: Sp.

title: Ethical Issues in Modern Medicine
catalogDescription: Case-based approach to ethical topics in medicine, such as abortion, genetic testing, physician-assisted death, and euthanasia. Emphasizes utilizing ethical principles and methods of case analysis. Offered: Sp.

effectiveYearRanges.note: Parsed from the official UW Seattle course descriptions page.

title: Bioethics: Secular and Jewish Perspectives
catalogDescription: Legal, ethical, scientific, and Jewish religious perspectives on contemporary medical and biomedical research practices. Legal and civil rights of women, people with disabilities, minors and minority or marginalized groups. Key differences between secular and Biblical/Rabbinic approaches in interpretation, analysis and application of bioethics, doctor-patient relationships; reproductive methods; abortion; euthanasia; and stem cell research. Offered: jointly with JEW ST 339/MELC 328.

title: History of Eugenics
catalogDescription: Examines the history of ideas, policies, and practices associated with eugenics and human genetics from the late nineteenth century to the present in American society and other national contexts. Offered: jointly with DIS ST 421.

title: Ethical Implications of Emerging Biotechnology
catalogDescription: Introduces students to select biotechnology innovations and invites consideration of the ethical and policy implications surrounding their development and potential use. Offered: W.
